metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before deleting `test_dispatch_coverage.py`, confirm whether its three enumerative guards (all pairs routed, distinct callables, unknown pair raises named error) are already asserted against `typed_dispatch._LEAVES` / `_apply_typed` — and if not, migrate them there as part of this plan so the safety net is not lost.
findings.finding: The plan prescribes deleting `test_dispatch_coverage.py` (or rewriting it to cover the live path) without verifying that the enumerative dispatch-completeness guards it contains — all `direction × action` pairs are routed, each leaf is distinct, and an unknown pair raises a named error — are also present for the real table (`typed_dispatch._LEAVES` / `_apply_typed`). A search of the test suite shows no equivalent enumerative-completeness test against `typed_dispatch`. Deleting the fossil tests without migrating these guards removes a whole-table safety net, not just dead-code coverage.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — '`test_dispatch_coverage.py` exercises the real dispatch path (or is removed)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a new test file (e.g. `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/orchestrate/test_typed_dispatch_coverage.py`) that mirrors the three test classes from `test_dispatch_coverage.py` but targets `typed_dispatch._LEAVES` and `_apply_typed`. Then the AC for deleting the fossil test is met without dropping the completeness guarantee.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `INVALID_PAIRS` (reconcile.py:36-49) to the explicit deletion targets in the plan, OR specify where it is relocated if the rewritten `test_dispatch_coverage.py` still needs a cartesian-coverage exclusion set.
findings.finding: The plan omits `INVALID_PAIRS` (reconcile.py:36) as a deletion/relocation target, even though it is a public symbol defined solely to support `test_dispatch_coverage.py`'s cartesian-product check and has no production caller. If `test_dispatch_coverage.py` is rewritten or deleted, `INVALID_PAIRS` becomes stranded dead code that the plan's AC does not address.
findings.location: Remediation section / AC line 1 ('dead dispatch table are removed') / src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py:36-49
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC: '`INVALID_PAIRS` is removed from `reconcile.py` (or relocated to the applier/mutation module if the rewritten test still needs a pair-exclusion set); no orphaned references remain.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `INVALID_PAIRS` (reconcile.py:36-49) is included in the deletion scope alongside `_dispatch_mutation` and `_DISPATCH_TABLE`, since it is defined solely to support `test_dispatch_coverage.py` and has no production callers.
findings.finding: `INVALID_PAIRS` (reconcile.py:36-49) is defined solely to support `test_dispatch_coverage.py`'s enumerative completeness check and has no production callers — its docstring explicitly states it exists 'so the enumerative coverage test can verify completeness of the dispatch table'. The plan's AC and remediation scope cover `_dispatch_mutation` and '_dispatch_table' but never mention `INVALID_PAIRS`, so the deletion will leave a stranded public symbol in reconcile.py.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a third bullet to the AC: '`INVALID_PAIRS` is removed from reconcile.py (no production callers; exists only to support the deleted coverage test).'

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `_build_dispatch_table` (reconcile.py:54-80) to the plan's explicit deletion targets alongside `_dispatch_mutation` and `_DISPATCH_TABLE`.
findings.finding: The plan does not explicitly name `_build_dispatch_table` (reconcile.py:54-80) as a deletion target, even though it is only ever called from `_dispatch_mutation` and exists solely to populate `_DISPATCH_TABLE`.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC line 1 to: '`_dispatch_mutation`, `_build_dispatch_table`, and the module-level `_DISPATCH_TABLE` variable are removed from `reconcile.py`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC, e.g.: AC #1: `grep -rn '_dispatch_mutation' src/ tests/` returns zero matches; AC #3: `coverage run -m pytest && coverage report | grep -v '_dispatch_mutation'` (or equivalent) shows no line hits for the deleted symbol.
findings.finding: No concrete proving commands are provided for any of the three ACs; in particular AC #3 ('Coverage no longer counts the fossil path') has no command that would produce evidence of success.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append a 'Verification' section:
```
# Verification
# AC1: symbol gone
grep -rn '_dispatch_mutation' src/ tests/  # expect: no output

# AC3: coverage clean
coverage run -m pytest
coverage report --include='src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py' | grep -v '_dispatch_mutation'
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default decision for `test_dispatch_coverage.py`: e.g., 'delete it unless a coverage gap on the live dispatch path is identified by running `coverage run -m pytest` and inspecting uncovered branches in `reconcile.py`'.
findings.finding: The remediation says 'rewrite/delete test_dispatch_coverage.py' and AC #2 says 'exercises the real dispatch path (or is removed)' — this is an OR without a decision criterion, deferring the design choice (rewrite vs. delete) to the executor.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a decision rule: 'Delete `test_dispatch_coverage.py`. If deleting it reveals an uncovered branch in the live dispatch path (identified via `coverage report`), add a targeted test for that branch instead.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that explicitly covers the cascade check: e.g. 'A grep/static-analysis scan confirms zero non-test callers of `_dispatch_mutation` and the dispatch table exist in the codebase prior to deletion.'
findings.finding: The `cascade_flag` in Remediation describes a required verification step ('confirm no non-test caller remains') that is a genuine deliverable, but no Acceptance Criterion covers it — leaving the implementer with no verifiable exit condition for this safety check.
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC4: 'A static analysis or grep scan of the full repository (excluding test files) confirms no remaining callers of `_dispatch_mutation` or the dispatch-table constant.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out plan (e.g., 'revert commit SHA X' or 'restore from branch `pre-deletion-backup`') or an expand-contract step (deprecation warning before hard deletion) so the destructive step is reversible.
findings.finding: The plan performs an irreversible code deletion but provides no explicit rollback or back-out plan; the cascade_flag is a pre-deletion check, not a recovery mechanism if the deletion is later found to be incorrect.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection: 'If a live caller is discovered post-merge, revert this PR via `git revert <merge-sha>`; the symbols are self-contained and the revert will be clean.' Alternatively, land the deletion behind a feature flag or in a separate commit tagged for easy revert.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `test_dispatch_coverage.py` should be deleted outright (preferred, given existing `_apply_typed` coverage in `test_inbound_leaf_bodies.py` and `test_apply_list_of_mutations.py`) or partially retained — specify the single intended outcome so the implementer has an unambiguous directive.
findings.finding: The plan says to 'rewrite/delete `test_dispatch_coverage.py` so coverage reflects the live dispatch path only', but the live path (`typed_dispatch._apply_typed` via `_LEAVES`) already has extensive test coverage across several existing test files. The plan does not specify whether the implementer should (a) delete the file, (b) add new tests to it covering `_apply_typed`/`_LEAVES` dispatch completeness, or (c) redirect assertions to the existing live-path tests. Without this decision, the implementer may add redundant tests or leave the file empty.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'rewrite/delete test_dispatch_coverage.py' with 'delete test_dispatch_coverage.py (the live dispatch path is already covered by test_inbound_leaf_bodies.py and test_apply_list_of_mutations.py; no new tests are needed for this change)'.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
